3A_Bibliographic.citation: Sanchez, M., Feldstein Ewing, S. W., & Luciana, M. (2023). Leveraging the Adolescent Brain Cognitive Development Study to advance and promote adolescent health: Introduction to the special issue. Health Psychology, 42(12), 835-838.
3A_Bibliographic.notes: This is an editorial introduction to a special issue, not an empirical study

3B_Sample_Data.n_analytic: N/A - editorial introduction, no original analysis
3B_Sample_Data.verbatim_sample_description: N/A - This paper is an introduction to a special issue summarizing other papers' contributions. It does not conduct original data analysis.

3C_Exposure.verbatim_exposure_description: N/A - Paper briefly mentions screen time as one of several topics covered by papers in the special issue but does not analyze screen time data.
3C_Exposure.notes: Screen time is mentioned only in passing when summarizing other papers in the special issue (e.g., Kiss et al., 2023; Mewton et al., 2023)

3E_Design_Analysis.study_design: editorial introduction / narrative review of special issue
3E_Design_Analysis.verbatim_analysis_description: N/A - No original data analysis. Paper provides an overview of contributions in the special issue.

3G_Reporting_Quality.notes: Not applicable - editorial introduction with no original empirical analysis

3H_Recommendations.notes: General editorial statement about ABCD study potential, no specific screen time recommendations

3I_Rhetorical_Moves.discussion_characterization: This is an editorial introduction to a special issue. It does not present or interpret screen time effect sizes.

3I_Flags.exclusion_flag: EXCLUDE - Editorial introduction to special issue, not an empirical study. No original data analysis of screen time effects.
3I_Flags.ambiguity_notes: Paper mentions screen time tangentially when summarizing special issue papers (Kiss et al., 2023 on COVID screen time changes; Mewton et al., 2023 on lifestyle risk factors and neurodevelopment). However, it contains no original empirical analysis and should be excluded from the systematic review.
